title: May 14, 2026AnnouncementsAnthropic forms $200 million partnership with the Gates Foundation
summary: Announcements Anthropic forms $200 million partnership with the Gates Foundation May 14, 2026 We’re partnering with the Gates Foundation to commit $200 million in grant funding, Claude usage credits, and technical support for programs in global health, life sciences, education, and economic mobility over the next four years. These programs will be implemented with partners in the US and around the world. This commitment is central to Anthropic’s efforts to extend the benefits of AI in areas where markets alone will not. This work is led by our Beneficial Deployments team, which provides Claude credits and engineering support to our partners in the four priority areas mentioned above. The team also develops AI-related public goods, such as public health datasets and evaluation benchmarks, and offers nonprofits and education institutions discounted access to Claude. We’re increasing our investment in beneficial deployments, and plan to share more about our approach to this work, and the impact of the programs we’ve supported. Below, we outline what’s involved in our partnership with the Gates Foundation, including our new initiatives and the work that's already underway. Global health and life sciences The largest part of our partnership will focus on improving health outcomes in low- and middle-income countries, where around 4.6 billion people lack access to essential health services. Anthropic will work with the Gates Foundation and others on a range of new and existing programs that will accelerate the development of new vaccines and therapies, and help governments use health data to make faster, better-informed decisions. As part of this work on healthcare intelligence, we’ll create connectors (which grant Claude direct access to other platforms and tools), benchmarks, and evaluation frameworks that allow researchers, developers, and governments to better understand how AI systems perform on healthcare-related tasks. In addition, we’ll work with the Gates Foundati
title_es: Anthropic destina 200 millones con Gates Foundation para IA en salud
bajada: Anthropic anunció una asociación de $200 millones con la Fundación Gates para extender los beneficios de Claude en salud global, ciencias de la vida, educación y movilidad económica. El programa incluye créditos de uso, soporte técnico y acceso preferencial para organizaciones sin fines de lucro.
bajada_en: Anthropic announced a $200 million partnership with the Gates Foundation to extend Claude's benefits in global health, life sciences, education, and economic mobility. The program includes usage credits, technical support, and discounted access for nonprofits.

title: Ontario auditors find doctors' AI note takers routinely blow basic facts
title_es: Auditores canadienses encuentran errores graves en IA médica
bajada: Auditores de Ontario descubrieron que herramientas de IA para tomar notas en consultas médicas cometen errores básicos sistemáticamente, incluyendo datos incorrectos. El hallazgo cuestiona la confiabilidad de IA en contextos críticos para la salud.
bajada_en: Ontario auditors found that AI medical note-taking tools routinely make basic errors, including incorrect data. The finding questions AI reliability in health-critical contexts.

title: arXiv implements 1-year ban for papers containing incontrovertible evidence of unchecked LLM-generated errors, such as hallucinated references or results. [N]
summary: From Thomas G. Dietterich (arXiv moderator for cs.LG) on 𝕏 (thread):https://x.com/tdietterich/status/2055000956144935055https://xcancel.com/tdietterich/status/2055000956144935055"Attention arXiv authors: Our Code of Conduct states that by signing your name as an author of a paper, each author takes full responsibility for all its contents, irrespective of how the contents were generated.If generative AI tools generate inappropriate language, plagiarized content, biased content, errors, mistakes, incorrect references, or misleading content, and that output is included in scientific works, it is the responsibility of the author(s).We have recently clarified our penalties for this. If a submission contains incontrovertible evidence that the authors did not check the results of LLM generation, this means we can't trust anything in the paper.The penalty is a 1-year ban from arXiv followed by the requirement that subsequent arXiv submissions must first be accepted at a reputable peer-reviewed venue.Examples of incontrovertible evidence: hallucinated references, meta-comments from the LLM ("here is a 200 word summary; would you like me to make any changes?"; "the data in this table is illustrative, fill it in with the real numbers from your experiments")."submitted by/u/Nunki08[link][comments]
title_es: arXiv implementa ban de 1 año por errores LLM no verificados
bajada: arXiv anunció que aplicará baneos de un año a autores cuyas publicaciones contengan evidencia de errores LLM no verificados, como referencias alucinadas. Es un paso importante para preservar integridad científica en era de generative AI.
bajada_en: arXiv announced one-year bans for papers with unchecked LLM-generated errors like hallucinated references. It's a significant step to preserve scientific integrity in the generative AI era.

title: Granite Embedding Multilingual R2: Open Apache 2.0 Multilingual Embeddings with 32K Context — Best Sub-100M Retrieval Quality
title_es: IBM lanza Granite Embedding multilingüe open-source con 32K tokens
bajada: Hugging Face anuncia Granite Embedding Multilingual R2, un modelo de embeddings open-source bajo licencia Apache 2.0 con soporte multilingüe y contexto de 32K tokens. Es una opción de bajo costo para retrieval quality sin exceder 100M parámetros.
bajada_en: Hugging Face announces Granite Embedding Multilingual R2, an open-source embedding model under Apache 2.0 license with multilingual support and 32K context window. It's a cost-effective option for retrieval quality under 100M parameters.

title: Runway started by helping filmmakers. Now it wants to beat Google at AI.
summary: AI video generation startup Runway is betting that video generation is the path to world models. And that being an AI outsider is an advantage, not a liability.
title_es: Runway apunta a ser competidor de Google en IA con generación de video
bajada: Runway, la startup de generación de video IA, está posicionándose para competir con Google apostando a que la generación de video es el camino hacia los world models. La empresa argumenta que su condición de outsider la posiciona mejor que los gigantes tech.
bajada_en: Runway, the AI video generation startup, is positioning itself to compete with Google by betting that video generation is the path to world models. The company argues its outsider status gives it an advantage over tech giants.

title: UK sovereign LLM inference
title_es: UK desarrolla capacidad soberana para inferencia de LLMs
bajada: Emerge iniciativas del Reino Unido para desarrollar infraestructura soberana de inferencia de LLMs, reflejando la tendencia global de gobiernos buscando independencia tecnológica en IA. Esto se alinea con objetivos de soberanía digital europeos.
bajada_en: The UK is developing sovereign LLM inference infrastructure, reflecting the global trend of governments seeking technological independence in AI. This aligns with European digital sovereignty objectives.

title: Show HN: GlycemicGPT – Open-source AI-powered diabetes management
summary:
I'm a Type 1 diabetic and software engineer. Last year I went months between endocrinologists with no clinician reviewing my data. I'm an engineer, so I built the tool I needed — and now I'm open sourcing it.
GlycemicGPT is a self-hosted platform that connects continuous glucose monitors, insulin pumps, and existing Nightscout instances to an AI analysis layer running on your own infrastructure.
Data sources:Dexcom G7 (cloud API)
Tandem t:slim X2 and Mobi pumps (direct BLE)
Nightscout (point it at your existing instance and you're running in minutes)What the AI layer does:Daily briefs summarizing overnight and 24-hour patterns
Meal response analysis
Conversational chat with RAG-backed clinical knowledge
Predictive alerting with configurable thresholds and caregiver escalationImportant: this is monitoring and analysis only. GlycemicGPT does not deliver insulin, does not control your pump, and is not a closed-loop system. It reads your data and gives you insight on top of it. Your clinical decisions stay between you and your care team.
Architecture:Self-hosted via Docker or K8S — the GlycemicGPT stack runs entirely on your hardware
BYOAI — bring your own AI provider. Use Ollama for fully local operation (no data leaves your hardware), or point it at Claude, OpenAI, or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint if you prefer a hosted model. Data flows directly from your instance to the provider you choose; nothing is routed through any centralized service operated by the project.
GPL-3.0, no subscriptions, no vendor lock-inStack:Backend API: FastAPI, Python 3.12, PostgreSQL 16, Redis 7
Web Dashboard: Next.js 15, React 19, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui
AI Sidecar: TypeScript, Express, multi-provider proxy
Android App: Kotlin, Jetpack Compose, BLE
Wear OS: Kotlin, Wear Compose, Watch Face Push API
Plugin SDK: Kotlin interfaces, capability-based, sandboxedLooking for contributors — especially folks with BLE/Android experience or anyone in the diabetes tech space. Plugin SDK is documented i
title_es: GlycemicGPT: IA open-source para gestión de diabetes
bajada: Un ingeniero software diabético lanzó GlycemicGPT, plataforma self-hosted que analiza datos de glucosa con IA, integrando monitores Dexcom y bombas de insulina. Es un ejemplo de IA healthcare personalizada y privada bajo control del usuario.
bajada_en: A software engineer with Type 1 diabetes launched GlycemicGPT, a self-hosted platform that analyzes glucose data with AI, integrating Dexcom monitors and insulin pumps. It's an example of personalized, private healthcare AI under user control.

title: Do Androids Dream of Breaking the Game? Systematically Auditing AI Agent Benchmarks with BenchJack
summary:
arXiv:2605.12673v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Agent benchmarks have become the de facto measure of frontier AI competence, guiding model selection, investment, and deployment. However, reward hacking, where agents maximize a score without performing the intended task, emerges spontaneously in frontier models without overfitting. We argue that benchmarks must be secure by design. From past incidents of reward hacks, we derive a taxonomy of eight recurring flaw patterns and compile them into the Agent-Eval Checklist for benchmark designers. We condense the insights into BenchJack, an automated red-teaming system that drives coding agents to audit benchmarks and identify possible reward-hacking exploits in a clairvoyant manner. Moreover, we extend BenchJack to an iterative generative-adversarial pipeline that discovers new flaws and patches them iteratively to improve benchmark robustness. We apply BenchJack to 10 popular agent benchmarks spanning software engineering, web navigation, desktop computing, and terminal operations. BenchJack synthesizes reward-hacking exploits that achieve near-perfect scores on most of the benchmarks without solving a single task, surfacing 219 distinct flaws across the eight classes. Moreover, BenchJack's extended pipeline reduces the hackable-task ratio from near 100% to under 10% on four benchmarks without fatal design flaws, fully patching WebArena and OSWorld within three iterations. Our results show that evaluation pipelines have not internalized an adversarial mindset, and that proactive auditing could help close the security gap for the fast-paced benchmarking space.
title_es: BenchJack expone vulnerabilidades en benchmarks de agentes IA
bajada: Un paper de arXiv presenta BenchJack, sistema de red-teaming que audita benchmarks de agentes y encuentra exploits de reward hacking. Descubrió 219 flaws en 10 benchmarks populares, cuestionando cómo medimos competencia de IA.
bajada_en: An arXiv paper introduces BenchJack, a red-teaming system that audits agent benchmarks and finds reward hacking exploits. It discovered 219 flaws in 10 popular benchmarks, questioning how we measure AI competence.

title: Work with Codex from anywhere
summary: Use Codex anywhere with the ChatGPT mobile app. Monitor, steer, and approve coding tasks in real time across devices and remote environments.
title_es: OpenAI permite usar Codex desde la app móvil de ChatGPT
bajada: OpenAI expandió el acceso a Codex permitiendo a los usuarios monitorear y aprobar tareas de coding en tiempo real desde la app móvil de ChatGPT en cualquier dispositivo. Esta integración simplifica el workflow para desarrolladores que trabajan en entornos remotos.
bajada_en: OpenAI expanded Codex access by allowing users to monitor and approve coding tasks in real-time from ChatGPT's mobile app on any device. This integration streamlines the workflow for developers working in remote environments.

title: Sea's View on the Future of Agentic Software Development with Codex
summary: Sea Limited's CPO explains why the company is deploying Codex across engineering teams to accelerate AI-native software development in Asia.
title_es: Sea Limited acelera desarrollo de software con Codex en Asia
bajada: Sea Limited está implementando Codex en sus equipos de ingeniería para impulsar el desarrollo de software nativo en IA. La estrategia refleja cómo empresas grandes en Asia están adoptando herramientas de IA para aumentar la productividad de desarrolladores.
bajada_en: Sea Limited is deploying Codex across engineering teams to accelerate AI-native software development. The move reflects how major Asian companies are adopting AI tools to boost developer productivity.

title: Osaurus brings both local and cloud AI models to your Mac
summary: Osaurus￼ combines local and cloud AI models in a Mac app that keeps users’ memory, files, and tools on their own hardware.
title_es: Osaurus trae modelos locales y en cloud a tu Mac
bajada: Osaurus lanzó una app para Mac que integra modelos de IA locales y en cloud, manteniendo memoria, archivos y herramientas del usuario en su propio hardware. Está dirigida a usuarios que requieren privacidad y control sobre sus datos de IA.
bajada_en: Osaurus launched a Mac app that integrates local and cloud AI models while keeping user memory, files, and tools on their own hardware. It targets users requiring privacy and control over their AI data.

title: Mira Murati Wants Her AI to ‘Keep Humans in the Loop’
summary: The Thinking Machines Lab founder and former CTO of OpenAI tells WIRED she isn’t interested in automating people out of jobs. Instead, she’s building AI that can collaborate.
title_es: Mira Murati construye IA colaborativa en Thinking Machines Lab
bajada: Mira Murati, ex-CTO de OpenAI y fundadora de Thinking Machines Lab, está desarrollando IA que mantiene a humanos en el loop colaborativo en lugar de automatizarlos. Su enfoque desafía la narrativa de automatización total en la industria.
bajada_en: Mira Murati, former OpenAI CTO and founder of Thinking Machines Lab, is developing AI that keeps humans in the collaborative loop rather than automating them out. Her approach challenges the full automation narrative in the industry.

title: How Claude Code works in large codebases
title_es: Claude Code publica guía para trabajar en codebases grandes
bajada: Anthropic publicó una guía detallada sobre cómo Claude Code funciona en proyectos grandes, incluyendo mejores prácticas y dónde empezar. Es recurso útil para desarrolladores que exploran capacidades de IA en codebases complejas.
bajada_en: Anthropic published a detailed guide on how Claude Code works in large projects, including best practices and getting started. It's a useful resource for developers exploring AI capabilities in complex codebases.

title: Show HN: Find the best local LLM for your hardware, ranked by benchmarks
title_es: Herramienta para encontrar el mejor LLM local según hardware
bajada: Un desarrollador lanzó un repositorio open-source que ayuda a usuarios a identificar el mejor LLM local para su hardware específico, rangado por benchmarks. Es útil para quien busca correr modelos localmente de forma eficiente.
bajada_en: A developer launched an open-source tool to help users identify the best local LLM for their specific hardware, ranked by benchmarks. It's useful for those looking to run models locally efficiently.

title: Claude for Legal
title_es: Anthropic publica Claude for Legal en GitHub
bajada: Anthropic lanzó Claude for Legal como proyecto open-source, proporcionando recursos y ejemplos para adaptar Claude a tareas legales. Es un ejemplo de especialización vertical de LLMs en sectores regulados.
bajada_en: Anthropic launched Claude for Legal as an open-source project, providing resources and examples to adapt Claude for legal tasks. It's an example of vertical LLM specialization in regulated sectors.
